short_questions.explanation: Frasa 'out of town' berarti sedang berada di luar kota. Karena mereka di luar kota sampai akhir bulan, mereka tidak akan bisa datang ke pesta tersebut.

options: She didn't read it at all
short_questions.explanation: Kata kerja 'skimmed through' berarti membaca secara sekilas atau cepat hanya untuk mendapatkan gagasan utamanya, bukan membaca secara detail.

options: She will do an excellent job
short_questions.explanation: Idiom 'knock it out of the park' berarti melakukan sesuatu dengan sangat spektakuler atau sukses besar.

options: She needs to change their lunch plans
She wants to invite someone else
short_questions.explanation: Idiom 'Something came up' berarti ada urusan mendadak yang terjadi. Ia kemudian meminta untuk menjadwalkan ulang (reschedule).

short_questions.questionText: What does the man agree to do?
short_questions.explanation: Frasa 'look over' berarti meninjau atau memeriksa. Pria itu setuju untuk memeriksa resume milik wanita tersebut.

options: She wants to buy a new jar
She will help him open the jar
She doesn't like what's inside the jar
short_questions.explanation: Idiom 'give it a try' berarti mencoba melakukan sesuatu. Ia menawarkan diri untuk mencoba membuka toples tersebut.

options: The weather will be clear and sunny
short_questions.explanation: Idiom 'won't be a cloud in the sky' (tidak akan ada satu awan pun di langit) menandakan bahwa cuaca besok akan sangat cerah.

options: She is surprised by the news
She helped him get the promotion
She was confident in his abilities
She is jealous of his promotion
short_questions.explanation: Idiom 'I knew you had it in you' berarti ia sudah tahu dari awal bahwa pria tersebut memiliki kemampuan untuk mencapainya (yakin padanya).

short_questions.questionText: What does the man tell the woman to do?
short_questions.explanation: Idiom 'Hold your horses' digunakan untuk menyuruh seseorang bersabar, menunggu sebentar, atau tidak terburu-buru.

short_questions.questionText: What does the woman say about the boss?
options: He will probably ignore the mistake
He wants to clean the office
short_questions.explanation: Idiom 'brush it off' berarti tidak mempedulikan, mengabaikan, atau menganggap sesuatu bukan masalah besar.

options: Students are attending a sporting event
short_questions.explanation: Pria itu mengatakan perpustakaan sepi karena semua orang sedang berada di stadion untuk menonton pertandingan sepak bola.

short_questions.questionText: What does the woman suggest the man do?
options: Stop working and go to sleep
short_questions.explanation: Idiom 'call it a night' berarti menghentikan aktivitas atau pekerjaan untuk hari itu dan pergi beristirahat/tidur.

options: He hasn't completed the assignment yet
He skipped the last two problems
short_questions.explanation: Pria itu menyatakan ia masih mengerjakan dua soal terakhir, yang berarti tugasnya belum selesai sepenuhnya.

options: He shouldn't go to the party
He should buy a new tie
short_questions.explanation: Wanita tersebut mengatakan pestanya 'casual' (santai), yang mengisyaratkan bahwa pria itu tidak perlu memakai pakaian formal (jas dan dasi).

options: Borrowing the book from the library
Sharing the book with a friend
short_questions.explanation: Pria itu menyarankan untuk membeli buku bekas (used copy) secara online untuk menghemat uang.

options: Suggests a possible location for the glasses
Offers to buy him new glasses
Tells him she broke his glasses
short_questions.explanation: Wanita tersebut memberikan saran atau lokasi spesifik (di meja dapur) tempat kacamata itu mungkin berada.

short_questions.questionText: What is the man's opinion of the exhibit?
options: It is mediocre and not very special
short_questions.explanation: Idiom 'nothing to write home about' digunakan untuk mendeskripsikan sesuatu yang biasa saja, tidak istimewa, atau kurang menarik.

options: She will make coffee for him
short_questions.explanation: Wanita itu memberi tahu bahwa ada mesin penjual otomatis (vending machine) di ujung lorong, menyiratkan bahwa pria itu bisa membeli kopi di sana.

short_questions.questionText: Why does the man agree to take the package?
options: He works at the post office
The post office is located along his route home
He is being paid to do it
short_questions.explanation: Pria itu setuju karena kantor pos searah dengan jalan pulangnya ('right on my way home').

short_questions.questionText: What does the woman suggest the man do?
short_questions.explanation: Wanita itu menyarankan pria tersebut untuk pergi ke sebelah dan meminta tetangganya secara sopan untuk mengecilkan volume ('go over and politely ask...').

options: He entirely forgot about the quiz
short_questions.explanation: Idiom 'slipped my mind' berarti lupa akan sesuatu secara menyeluruh.

options: She wants the man to pay for everything
She insists on paying for the whole meal
short_questions.explanation: Idiom 'my treat' berarti seseorang menawarkan diri untuk membayar tagihan (mentraktir) orang lain.

options: He is an expert at reading maps
He regrets that they didn't bring a map
He knows exactly where they are
He wants to buy a map
short_questions.explanation: Pola kalimat 'should have + V3' (should have brought) menunjukkan penyesalan atas tindakan yang tidak dilakukan di masa lalu (menyesal tidak membawa peta).

options: She does not have time to talk
She doesn't know what time it is
She wants to buy a new watch
She is late for an appointment
short_questions.explanation: Karena jam tangannya rusak ('watch is broken'), ia menyiratkan bahwa ia tidak bisa memberi tahu pukul berapa sekarang.

short_questions.questionText: What does the man say about his sister?
options: She is climbing a tall building
short_questions.explanation: Idiom 'on top of the world' digunakan untuk menggambarkan perasaan sangat bahagia dan gembira.

options: Taking chairs from a nearby room
Moving the seminar to another room
short_questions.explanation: Wanita tersebut mengusulkan solusi dengan meminjam kursi dari ruang kelas kosong di sebelah ('borrow some from the empty classroom next door').

options: He stayed until the very end
He did not finish watching the play
He met the actors during the break
short_questions.explanation: Karena pria itu pulang saat jeda istirahat tengah pertunjukan ('intermission'), berarti ia tidak menonton sampai selesai (kemungkinan karena tidak menyukainya).

short_questions.questionText: What will the woman probably do?
options: Turn down the volume of the TV
short_questions.explanation: Karena ia meminta maaf dan baru menyadari TV-nya terlalu keras, sangat masuk akal bahwa tindakan selanjutnya adalah mengecilkan volume TV tersebut.

options: He tells her to reject the job
He reassures her that her feelings are normal
He offers her a different job
short_questions.explanation: Pria itu menenangkannya dengan mengatakan bahwa merasa gugup ('nervous') sebelum memulai hal baru adalah hal yang sangat normal (memberikan penegasan/reassurance).

options: The mail will not come today
The mail has not arrived yet, but will soon
The man should go to the post office
short_questions.explanation: Karena wanita itu menyebutkan bahwa tukang pos biasanya datang sekitar tengah hari (noon), ia mengisyaratkan bahwa saat ini surat belum tiba, tetapi akan segera datang.

options: The software is impossible to use
She should hang up the phone
She will learn how to use it with practice
She needs to buy a new computer
short_questions.explanation: Idiom 'get the hang of it' berarti pada akhirnya akan mengerti cara menggunakan atau melakukan sesuatu setelah berlatih beberapa waktu.

options: There is no need to rush
short_questions.explanation: Wanita itu menyuruhnya rileks karena ada iklan/cuplikan ('previews') selama 15 menit sebelum film asli dimulai, yang berarti mereka masih punya waktu (tidak perlu terburu-buru).

options: He had already heard the news
short_questions.explanation: Idiom 'Word travels fast' digunakan untuk menyatakan bahwa rumor, berita, atau informasi menyebar dengan sangat cepat (berarti dia sudah mendengar berita itu).

options: She hasn't seen the figures yet
She is actively investigating the problem
She doesn't care about the sales
She thinks the man is wrong
short_questions.explanation: Wanita tersebut mengatakan ia telah berusaha mencari tahu apa yang salah sepanjang pagi ('trying to figure out what went wrong'), yang berarti ia sedang aktif menyelidiki masalah penurunan penjualan tersebut.

short_questions.questionText: What does the man suggest the woman do?
options: Ask the professor for the exam
short_questions.explanation: Pria itu menyuruh wanita tersebut mempertimbangkan kembali keputusannya ('reconsider') untuk absen, karena profesor akan memberikan petunjuk ujian. Ini berarti pria itu menyarankan agar wanita tersebut tetap hadir di sesi ulasan (review session).

questions.questionText: What is the main purpose of the conversation?
options: To discuss a new recycling program
To apply for a student government position
questions.explanation: Para mahasiswa sedang membahas implementasi 'a new comprehensive recycling program on campus'.

questions.questionText: According to the man, what is the biggest challenge to the program?
options: Finding a space for the bins
questions.explanation: Pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa 'generating interest among the student body is our greatest hurdle'.

questions.questionText: What does the woman suggest to improve student participation?
options: Holding a recycling competition between dorms
Giving out free coffee to recyclers
questions.explanation: Dia menyarankan, 'What if we had a competition to see which dorm can recycle the most?'

questions.questionText: What will the man do next?
options: Draft a proposal for the competition
questions.explanation: Di akhir, dia berkata, 'I'll get started on drafting that proposal right away'.

questions.questionText: What is the main topic of the lecture?
options: The formation of the solar system
The differences between comets and asteroids
The distance from Earth to Mars
questions.explanation: Profesor menyatakan bahwa kuliah hari ini akan membahas perbedaan antara komet dan asteroid: 'Today we are going to clarify the difference between... comets and asteroids.'

questions.questionText: How does the professor describe a comet?
questions.explanation: Profesor secara eksplisit berkata: 'You can think of a comet as a dirty snowball.'

questions.questionText: Where are most asteroids located?
questions.explanation: Pembicaraan menyebutkan: 'Most of them are found in the Asteroid Belt, a vast ring located between the orbits of Mars and Jupiter.'

questions.questionText: What happens when a comet gets close to the Sun?
questions.explanation: Profesor menjelaskan bahwa es memanas 'creating a glowing atmosphere and a tail'.

questions.questionText: What prevented people from using early elevators for passengers?
options: They were dangerous if the cable broke
They were only allowed in factories
questions.explanation: Pemandu menjelaskan bahwa jika kabel putus, lift akan jatuh, sehingga orang takut menggunakannya: 'If the lifting cable snapped, the car would crash... people were afraid to use them.'

questions.questionText: What did Elisha Otis invent?
questions.explanation: Pemandu menjelaskan: 'He invented a safety brake.'

questions.questionText: How did Otis demonstrate his invention?
options: He ordered the rope to be cut while standing on the elevator
He built the Empire State Building
He wrote a book about it
He dropped a heavy box from a tower
questions.explanation: Teks menyatakan: 'He stood on an elevator platform and ordered the rope to be cut.'

questions.questionText: What was the result of this invention?
options: It allowed for the construction of skyscrapers
It caused the World's Fair to close
It reduced the cost of steel
questions.explanation: Pemandu menyimpulkan: 'This invention gave architects the confidence to design tall buildings.'

questions.questionText: What is the main topic of the lecture?
options: The life cycle of a queen bee
questions.explanation: Profesor membahas bagaimana lebah berkomunikasi menggunakan 'waggle dance' untuk berbagi informasi tentang makanan.

questions.questionText: What is the 'waggle dance' used for?
options: To indicate distance and direction of food
questions.explanation: Profesor berkata: 'This dance tells the other bees two things: the distance and the direction of the food.'

questions.questionText: How do bees indicate direction?
options: By the loudness of their buzz
By the angle of the dance relative to the sun
questions.explanation: Pembicaraan menyatakan: 'The angle of the dance relates to the position of the sun, indicating direction.'

questions.questionText: What determines the distance to the food source?
questions.explanation: Profesor menjelaskan: 'The duration of the waggle part of the dance indicates how far away the flowers are.'

questions.questionText: What characterized art before Impressionism?
options: It was realistic and highly detailed
questions.explanation: Profesor berkata: 'Before this, art was expected to be realistic and highly detailed.'

questions.questionText: What did Impressionists want to capture?
options: The momentary effect of light and color
The anatomy of the human body
questions.explanation: They 'wanted to capture the momentary effect of light and color.'

questions.questionText: What does 'en plein air' mean?
questions.explanation: Profesor mendefinisikannya sebagai: 'en plein air, or outdoors.'

questions.questionText: How did critics initially react to Impressionism?
options: They hated it and called it unfinished
questions.explanation: Teks menyatakan: 'At first, critics hated it, calling the works unfinished sketches.'

questions.questionText: How does the speaker describe the Earth's outer shell?
questions.explanation: The speaker says it is 'broken into large pieces called tectonic plates.'

questions.questionText: How fast do tectonic plates move?
questions.explanation: Pembicara membandingkan kecepatannya dengan: 'about the same speed that your fingernails grow.'

questions.questionText: What happens when plates interact at their boundaries?
questions.explanation: Pembicara menyebutkan bahwa interaksi lempeng menyebabkan bencana alam: 'they cause earthquakes, volcanic eruptions...'

questions.questionText: What is the theory called?
questions.explanation: Pembicara secara eksplisit menyatakan: 'This theory is called Plate Tectonics.'

questions.questionText: Why is the student visiting the professor?
options: To ask for a grade change
To discuss his research paper topic
questions.explanation: Mahasiswa berkata: 'I wanted to discuss my topic for the final research paper.'

questions.questionText: What was the student's initial topic idea?
options: Impact of social media on marketing
questions.explanation: Dia mengusulkan: 'analyzing the impact of social media on modern marketing.'

questions.questionText: What was the professor's main feedback?
options: The student should change his major
The topic is perfect as is
questions.explanation: Profesor memberikan saran untuk mempersempit topik yang terlalu luas: 'it is also extremely broad... I suggest you narrow it down.'

questions.questionText: How did the student narrow down the topic?
options: By focusing on Instagram and small businesses
questions.explanation: Dia menyarankan: 'focus specifically on how small businesses use Instagram.'

questions.questionText: What is a coral actually composed of?
questions.explanation: Profesor menjelaskan: 'it is actually a colony of tiny animals called polyps.'

questions.questionText: What is the relationship between coral and zooxanthellae?
questions.explanation: Kuliah menyatakan: 'These polyps have a fascinating symbiotic relationship with microscopic algae.'

questions.questionText: What happens during 'coral bleaching'?
options: The coral turns white because it expels the algae
The coral moves to colder water
questions.explanation: Karang membuang alga simbiotiknya sehingga berubah menjadi putih: 'corals... expel these algae. This causes the coral to turn completely white.'

questions.questionText: What is the main cause of coral bleaching mentioned in the lecture?
questions.explanation: Penyebab utama pemutihan karang adalah kenaikan suhu air: 'when water temperatures rise due to climate change...'

questions.questionText: What is the main topic of the lecture?
options: The difference between short-term and long-term memory
questions.explanation: Profesor memperkenalkan topik: 'The most common model divides memory into two main types: short-term and long-term memory.'

questions.questionText: To what does the professor compare short-term memory?
questions.explanation: Profesor secara eksplisit berkata: 'It's like a computer's RAM.'

questions.questionText: About how long does information stay in short-term memory without rehearsal?
questions.explanation: Teks menyatakan: 'You can hold information there for about 20 to 30 seconds.'

questions.questionText: What is required to move information into long-term memory?
questions.explanation: Profesor menjelaskan: 'you need to use a process called rehearsal or encoding.'

questions.questionText: Why did the student visit the career counselor?
options: To find a job after graduation
To get help with his resume for an internship
questions.explanation: Mahasiswa berkata: 'I'm planning to apply for summer internships, but I'm really struggling with my resume.'

questions.questionText: What is the student worried about regarding his resume?
options: It is too empty because he lacks experience
The font size is too small
questions.explanation: Dia menyebutkan: 'I feel like it's too empty. I haven't had a real job before.'

questions.questionText: What does the counselor say about volunteer work?
options: It does not count as experience
It counts as experience and shows dedication
It should be removed from the resume
It is only useful for scholarships
questions.explanation: Dia meyakinkannya: 'volunteer work counts as experience! It shows dedication and skill.'

questions.questionText: What does the counselor suggest the student do next?
options: Attend a Resume Workshop on Thursday
questions.explanation: Dia merekomendasikan: 'I recommend you attend our Resume Workshop this Thursday.'

questions.questionText: What is the main difference between Realism and Romanticism mentioned by the professor?
options: Realism depicts life as it is, while Romanticism focuses on idealism
Realism focuses on poetry, while Romanticism focuses on novels
questions.explanation: Profesor menyatakan: 'While Romanticism focused on emotion and idealism, Realism tried to depict life exactly as it was.'

questions.questionText: Why does the professor mention Mark Twain?
options: He was a central figure of the Realism movement
questions.explanation: Teks berkata: 'A central figure of this movement was Mark Twain.'

questions.questionText: What was unique about the dialogue in 'Huckleberry Finn'?
options: It used the specific dialect of the American South
questions.explanation: Profesor menjelaskan penggunaan dialek lokal oleh Mark Twain agar karakter terdengar nyata: 'Twain used the specific dialect of the American South... he made them speak like real people.'

questions.questionText: What subjects did Realist writers focus on?
questions.explanation: Kuliah menyimpulkan: 'Realist writers focused on everyday events, common people, and social problems.'

questions.questionText: Where does geothermal energy come from?
questions.explanation: Pembicara menyatakan: 'This energy comes from the heat stored inside the Earth.'

questions.questionText: What is the main advantage of geothermal energy over solar and wind?
options: It is consistent and available 24 hours a day
questions.explanation: Pembicara menekankan: 'One of the biggest advantages... is consistency... available 24 hours a day.'

questions.questionText: Why do solar panels and wind turbines have limitations according to the talk?
options: They depend on weather conditions (sun and wind)
questions.explanation: Pembicara mencatat: 'Solar panels don't work at night, and wind turbines stop when the air is still.'

questions.questionText: Where are geothermal resources most commonly found?
options: In the middle of the desert
Along major plate boundaries like the 'Ring of Fire'
questions.explanation: Teks menyebutkan: 'found along major plate boundaries... such as the Ring of Fire.'

questions.questionText: What is the student's problem?
options: Her roommate has a conflicting schedule
questions.explanation: Dia menjelaskan: 'my roommate and I have very different schedules... She studies late at night... and I have 8 AM classes.'

questions.questionText: What is the 'freeze period'?
options: A time when the heating is turned off
The first two weeks when no moves are allowed
A time when students must stay indoors
questions.explanation: Petugas mendefinisikannya sebagai: 'a freeze period for the first two weeks of classes where no moves are allowed.'

questions.questionText: Why does the university have a freeze period?
options: To verify who actually arrived on campus
questions.explanation: Petugas berkata: 'This gives us time to verify who actually arrived on campus.'

questions.questionText: What can the student do today?
options: Move to a new room immediately
Fill out a transfer request form
questions.explanation: Petugas menyimpulkan: 'You can fill out this transfer request form today, but we can't process the move until next Monday.'

questions.questionText: What is the main topic of the lecture?
questions.explanation: The professor introduces the topic: 'This brings us to the concept of mimicry.'

questions.questionText: Why do some harmless animals mimic dangerous ones?
questions.explanation: The text states it is 'to scare off predators' by looking dangerous.

questions.questionText: What is unique about the Viceroy butterfly?
options: It mimics the toxic Monarch butterfly
questions.explanation: The professor says: 'It looks almost identical to the Monarch butterfly.'

questions.questionText: Why do birds avoid Monarch butterflies?
options: They are toxic and taste terrible
questions.explanation: The text explains: 'Birds avoid Monarchs because they are toxic and taste terrible.'

questions.questionText: What was the Industrial Revolution?
options: A war between Britain and France
The transition from hand tools to machines
questions.explanation: The professor defines it as: 'the transition from creating goods by hand to using machines.'

questions.questionText: Where did the Industrial Revolution begin?
questions.explanation: The text states it 'began in Britain in the late 18th century.'

questions.questionText: Which invention is mentioned as a key factor in this revolution?
questions.explanation: The professor says: 'The invention of the steam engine changed everything.'

questions.questionText: What was a social effect of the Industrial Revolution mentioned in the talk?
options: Rapid urbanization and overcrowding in cities
questions.explanation: It 'led to rapid urbanization... it also created difficult living conditions in overcrowded cities.'

questions.questionText: What is the main subject of the lecture?
questions.explanation: The professor discusses 'the first practical photographic process, known as the daguerreotype.'

questions.questionText: What was the major disadvantage of early photography?
options: The exposure time was very long
It could only take pictures of landscapes
questions.explanation: The professor notes: 'the exposure time was very long. A subject had to sit perfectly still for 10 to 15 minutes.'

questions.questionText: Why did people in old portraits often look stiff and serious?
options: It was the fashion of the time
They had to sit still for a long time
questions.explanation: The text explains: 'they just couldn't move a muscle while the camera captured the light.'

questions.questionText: Who introduced the daguerreotype process?
questions.explanation: The lecture states: 'Louis Daguerre introduced the first practical photographic process.'

questions.questionText: Why did the student go to the library?
options: Because he received an email about an overdue book
questions.explanation: He says: 'I received an email saying I have an overdue book.'

questions.questionText: Why was the student confused about the due date?
options: He didn't realize it was a Reserve book with a 24-hour limit
He thought the library was closed
questions.explanation: The librarian explains: 'This book was from the Reserve section... usually just 24 hours.'

questions.questionText: What is the fine policy for Reserve books?
questions.explanation: The librarian states: 'It is one dollar for every hour it is late.'

questions.questionText: What must the student do before borrowing more books?
questions.explanation: The librarian says: 'You'll need to pay it before you can borrow anything else.'

questions.questionText: What is the defining characteristic of a black hole?
options: Its gravity is so strong that light cannot escape
questions.explanation: The professor defines it as a region 'where the pulling force of gravity is so strong that light is not able to escape.'

questions.questionText: How is a black hole formed?
options: When a massive star dies and collapses
When a comet hits the sun
questions.explanation: The text says: 'This can happen when a massive star dies and collapses in on itself.'

questions.questionText: Why are black holes invisible to the naked eye?
options: No light can get out of them
questions.explanation: The professor explains: 'Because no light can get out, people can't see black holes.'

questions.questionText: What is the 'event horizon'?
options: The center of the black hole
The boundary from which nothing can escape
questions.explanation: It is described as 'The boundary around a black hole from which nothing can escape.'

questions.questionText: Who conducted the famous experiment discussed in the lecture?
questions.explanation: The professor mentions: 'One of the most famous experiments... was conducted by Ivan Pavlov.'

questions.questionText: What animals were used in the experiment?
questions.explanation: The text states: 'He was studying digestion in dogs.'

questions.questionText: What did the dogs learn to associate with food?
questions.explanation: Pavlov rang a bell, and 'Eventually, the dogs learned to associate the sound of the bell with food.'

questions.questionText: What is the name of this learning process?
questions.explanation: The lecture concludes: 'This process of learning through association is called Classical Conditioning.'

questions.questionText: What is desalination?
options: Removing salt from seawater to make it drinkable
questions.explanation: The speaker defines it as: 'The process of removing salt and minerals from seawater to make it drinkable.'

questions.questionText: What is 'reverse osmosis'?
options: Pushing water through a filter to catch salt
questions.explanation: The text explains it as: 'water is pushed through a filter that catches the salt.'

questions.questionText: What is a major disadvantage of desalination mentioned in the talk?
questions.explanation: The speaker notes: 'It is very expensive and requires a massive amount of energy.'

questions.questionText: Where is this technology particularly critical?
options: In dry regions like the Middle East
questions.explanation: The text mentions it provides 'a critical water source for dry regions like the Middle East.'

questions.questionText: In the Potlatch ceremony, how was wealth defined?
options: By how much gold one saved
By the size of one's house
By how much one gave away
By the number of children one had
questions.explanation: The professor states: 'wealth wasn't defined by how much you saved, but by how much you gave away.'

questions.questionText: Where did this custom originate?
options: Pacific Northwest Coast of North America
questions.explanation: The text mentions: 'indigenous cultures of the Pacific Northwest Coast of North America.'

questions.questionText: What happens during a Potlatch?
options: A host distributes gifts to guests
A leader is elected by voting
questions.explanation: The professor explains: 'A Potlatch is a lavish feast where the host distributes gifts to the guests.'

questions.questionText: What was the social purpose of the Potlatch?
options: To establish social status and redistribute wealth
questions.explanation: It was a way to 'redistribute wealth and establish social hierarchy.'

questions.questionText: What event are the students discussing?
questions.explanation: The woman asks: 'Hey, are you going to the Student Club Fair today?'

questions.questionText: Where is the event taking place?
questions.explanation: She says: 'It's on the main Quad, right in front of the library.'

questions.questionText: Which club is the woman interested in joining?
questions.explanation: She states: 'I'm thinking of joining the Photography Club.'

questions.questionText: What does the man want to do?
questions.explanation: He says: 'I've been wanting to play chess again. Do you know if there's a chess club?'

questions.questionText: What force primarily carved the Grand Canyon?
questions.explanation: The professor states: 'it was carved primarily by a single force: water... the Colorado River.'

questions.questionText: Why is the Grand Canyon valuable to geologists?
options: The exposed rock layers provide a timeline of Earth's history
It is the deepest hole on Earth
It is home to rare birds
questions.explanation: The text explains: 'these exposed layers provide a timeline of Earth's history.'

questions.questionText: How old are the bottom layers of rock in the canyon?
questions.explanation: The professor notes: 'The bottom rocks are billions of years old.'

questions.questionText: How does the professor describe looking at the canyon?
questions.explanation: The lecture concludes: 'It’s like looking back in time just by looking down.'

questions.questionText: What is the main topic of the lecture?
options: How to dive safely in the dark
questions.explanation: The professor discusses 'bioluminescence' and how 'Deep-sea creatures use this ability for survival'.

questions.questionText: According to the professor, how do some fish use bioluminescence to hunt?
options: By seeing better in the dark
By using it as a lure to attract prey
questions.explanation: The text states: 'some fish use it as a lure to attract prey, like the anglerfish'.

questions.questionText: What is 'counter-illumination'?
options: A way to communicate with mates
A form of camouflage to blend with light from above
A chemical reaction that creates heat
questions.explanation: The professor explains it as 'producing light on their bellies that matches the faint sunlight from above... a trick called counter-illumination'.

questions.questionText: Besides hunting and camouflage, what is the third use of bioluminescence mentioned?
options: To stay warm in freezing water
questions.explanation: The lecture concludes: 'Finally, bioluminescence is used to communicate, helping these animals find mates'.

questions.questionText: What is the main topic of the lecture?
options: How to write in ancient Greek
The discovery and significance of the Rosetta Stone
questions.explanation: The professor focuses on the discovery of the Rosetta Stone and how it helped crack the code of hieroglyphs.

questions.questionText: Who discovered the Rosetta Stone?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states it was discovered 'in 1799 when French soldiers under Napoleon discovered a heavy, dark rock'.

questions.questionText: Why was the Rosetta Stone so important to historians?
options: It was made of pure gold
It contained a map of ancient Egypt
It had the same text written in three different scripts
questions.explanation: The professor says: 'What made this stone so incredibly important was that it contained the exact same royal decree written in three different scripts'.

questions.questionText: What did the Rosetta Stone allow scholars to finally do?
options: Understand and read ancient Egyptian hieroglyphs
questions.explanation: The breakthrough allowed them to crack the code and 'unlocked thousands of years of ancient Egyptian history'.

questions.questionText: Why is the student talking to the academic advisor?
options: To change his major to Spanish
To ask about funding for a study abroad program
questions.explanation: The student says he wants to study in Spain 'but I'm worried about the travel and housing costs'.

questions.questionText: What specific grant does the advisor recommend?
questions.explanation: The advisor asks: 'Have you looked into the Global Scholar Grant?'.

questions.questionText: When is the deadline to apply for the grant?
questions.explanation: The advisor states: 'The deadline to apply is next month, on the 15th.'

questions.questionText: What does the student need to submit along with the online form?
options: Two letters of recommendation from professors
questions.explanation: The advisor says he must 'submit two letters of recommendation from your professors.'

questions.questionText: What does REM stand for?
questions.explanation: The professor explains: 'REM sleep, which stands for Rapid Eye Movement.'

questions.questionText: What happens during REM sleep?
options: Our body temperature drops to freezing
questions.explanation: The text states: 'This is the stage where our most vivid dreams occur.'

questions.questionText: How is the brain's activity described during this stage?
options: It is highly active, almost like being awake
It works at half its normal speed
questions.explanation: The professor says: 'your brain is highly active, almost as active as when you are awake.'

questions.questionText: Why does the body go into a state of temporary paralysis during REM sleep?
options: To save energy for the next day
To allow the heart to rest
To prevent people from physically acting out their dreams
questions.explanation: The professor explains that paralysis 'prevents you from physically acting out your dreams, which could lead to you getting injured.'

questions.questionText: What is the Urban Heat Island effect?
options: The tendency for cities to flood easily
A phenomenon where cities are significantly warmer than rural areas
The construction of tropical resorts in cities
A type of pollution caused by cars
questions.explanation: The professor defines it as: 'Cities are significantly warmer than surrounding rural areas.'

questions.questionText: According to the lecture, what materials contribute heavily to this effect?
questions.explanation: The text mentions: 'Cities are built with concrete, asphalt, and brick. These materials absorb massive amounts of heat.'

questions.questionText: How do plants and trees naturally cool an area?
options: By releasing cool air from their roots
Through shade and the evaporation of water from their leaves
questions.explanation: The professor states: 'Plants provide natural cooling through shade and the evaporation of water from their leaves.'

questions.questionText: What is one solution city planners are using to combat urban heat?
questions.explanation: The lecture concludes: 'many city planners are now installing green roofs—roofs covered with plants'.

questions.questionText: What is the main topic of the lecture?
options: How to perform first aid in an emergency
A phenomenon where people are less likely to help when others are present
How to train bystanders to be more helpful
questions.explanation: Profesor mendefinisikan 'Bystander Effect' sebagai 'kasus di mana individu tidak menawarkan bantuan apa pun kepada korban ketika ada orang lain'.

questions.questionText: According to research, what happens to the probability of help as the number of bystanders increases?
options: It depends on the time of day
questions.explanation: Teks menyatakan: 'probabilitas bantuan berbanding terbalik dengan jumlah saksi. Dengan kata lain... semakin kecil kemungkinan salah satu dari mereka untuk membantu.'

questions.questionText: What psychological concept explains this phenomenon?
questions.explanation: Profesor menjelaskan: 'Para psikolog mengaitkan hal ini dengan konsep yang disebut difusi tanggung jawab (diffusion of responsibility).'

questions.questionText: What might a person think if there are many people witnessing an emergency?
options: I must be the first one to help.
Someone else has probably already called for help.
This is not a real emergency.
I should record this on my phone.
questions.explanation: Profesor memberikan contoh proses pemikiran tersebut: 'Orang lain mungkin sudah menelepon untuk meminta bantuan'.

questions.questionText: Where is the Kuiper Belt located?
options: Between the Earth and the Moon
Near the center of the Sun
questions.explanation: Kuliah dimulai dengan: 'Di luar orbit Neptunus terletak wilayah besar berbentuk donat... yang dikenal sebagai Sabuk Kuiper.'

questions.questionText: How does the Kuiper Belt compare to the asteroid belt?
options: It is much smaller and less massive.
It is exactly the same size.
It is made entirely of hot gas.
It is far larger and much more massive.
questions.explanation: Profesor mengatakan: Sabuk Kuiper 'jauh lebih besar—sekitar 20 kali lebih lebar dan 20 hingga 200 kali lebih masif' daripada sabuk asteroid.

questions.questionText: What are objects in the Kuiper Belt primarily composed of?
options: Frozen volatiles like methane, ammonia, and water
questions.explanation: Teks menyatakan: 'Objek Sabuk Kuiper sebagian besar terdiri dari zat volatil beku, seperti metana, amonia, dan air.'

questions.questionText: Why was Pluto reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006?
options: Because it left the solar system
Because other similar-sized objects were discovered in the Kuiper Belt
Because it moved closer to the Sun
questions.explanation: Profesor menjelaskan bahwa 'penemuannya, bersama dengan objek berukuran serupa lainnya di sabuk tersebut, akhirnya membuat para astronom mengklasifikasikannya ulang'.

questions.questionText: Why is the student visiting the registrar's office?
options: Because the online system won't let him register for an Economics class
To change his major to Economics
questions.explanation: Mahasiswa tersebut mengatakan: 'Saya mencoba mendaftar untuk seminar lanjutan di bidang Ekonomi... tetapi sistem online tidak mengizinkan saya.'

questions.questionText: What is the reason the system is blocking the student?
options: He owes money to the university
He hasn't completed the prerequisite course according to the system
questions.explanation: Petugas mengatakan kepadanya: 'Sistem memblokir Anda karena Anda belum menyelesaikan mata kuliah prasyarat, Econ 305.'

questions.questionText: Why does the student believe he should be allowed to take the class?
options: He is a senior and needs it to graduate
He took an equivalent course while studying abroad in London
He has permission from the professor
questions.explanation: Mahasiswa tersebut beralasan: 'Tapi saya mengambil kursus yang setara ketika saya belajar di luar negeri di London semester lalu.'

questions.questionText: What must the student do to resolve the issue?
options: Wait for the system to update automatically
Get a prerequisite override form signed by the Economics Department chair
Call his previous university in London
questions.explanation: Petugas menginstruksikan: 'Anda harus mendapatkan formulir pembatalan prasyarat (prerequisite override form) yang ditandatangani oleh ketua Departemen Ekonomi.'

questions.questionText: What old scientific belief has been overturned by recent studies?
options: That birds cannot fly long distances
That only humans and primates are capable of making and using tools
That crows are related to reptiles
That animals cannot communicate with each other
questions.explanation: Profesor menyebutkan bahwa keyakinan 'bahwa manusia dan beberapa primata yang berkerabat dekat adalah satu-satunya hewan yang mampu membuat dan menggunakan alat' telah terbantahkan.

questions.questionText: Which specific bird is discussed in the lecture?
questions.explanation: Kuliah tersebut secara spesifik menyebutkan 'Gagak Kaledonia Baru (New Caledonian crow)'.

questions.questionText: How do these crows improve the twigs they find in the wild?
options: They sharpen the ends on rocks
They bend them into hooks using their beaks and feet
They tie them together with grass
They coat them in poisonous sap
questions.explanation: Profesor menjelaskan: 'Mereka akan mengambil ranting lurus dan membengkokkannya menjadi kail menggunakan paruh dan kaki mereka.'

questions.questionText: What have laboratory experiments revealed about these birds?
options: They can solve complex, multi-step puzzles to access food
They prefer eating fruit over insects
They cannot use tools outside of the wild
questions.explanation: Teks menyatakan: 'eksperimen laboratorium telah menunjukkan bahwa mereka dapat memecahkan teka-teki multi-langkah yang kompleks untuk mengakses makanan'.

questions.questionText: What impact did the Transcontinental Railroad have on travel time to California?
options: It reduced it from months to just one week
It reduced it from years to months
questions.explanation: Profesor mengatakan: 'Kereta api mengurangi waktu perjalanan ini menjadi hanya satu minggu' (dari perjalanan yang sebelumnya memakan waktu berbulan-bulan).

questions.questionText: How was the railroad constructed?
options: By a single company building from East to West
By the military during a war
By two companies building towards each other from opposite directions
By importing pre-built tracks from Europe
questions.explanation: Teks menjelaskan bahwa proyek ini melibatkan 'dua perusahaan: Union Pacific yang membangun ke arah barat... dan Central Pacific yang membangun ke arah timur'.

questions.questionText: Where did the two rail lines meet?
questions.explanation: Profesor secara eksplisit menyatakan: 'Mereka bertemu di Promontory Summit di Utah'.

questions.questionText: What object was used ceremonially to connect the two lines?
questions.explanation: Kuliah tersebut menyebutkan 'Paku Emas (Golden Spike) seremonial ditancapkan ke jalur tersebut'.

questions.questionText: What is the main topic of the lecture?
options: A mental state called cognitive dissonance
questions.explanation: Profesor menjelaskan tentang 'Disonansi Kognitif', yaitu kondisi mental tidak nyaman akibat dua keyakinan yang bertentangan.

questions.questionText: According to the professor, why is a smoker often in a state of dissonance?
options: Because their habit conflicts with their knowledge of its dangers
Because they prefer to smoke alone
Because they are forced to quit by law
questions.explanation: Perokok mengalami disonansi karena perilaku mereka (merokok) bertentangan dengan pengetahuan mereka bahwa merokok itu berbahaya.

questions.questionText: What do humans tend to do to reduce the discomfort of dissonance?
options: They change their beliefs or behavior to make them consistent
They try to experience more discomfort
They stop thinking about the future
questions.explanation: Teks menyatakan bahwa manusia cenderung mengubah salah satu (keyakinan atau perilaku) agar keduanya sejalan.

questions.questionText: What is an example of changing a belief to reduce dissonance mentioned in the talk?
options: Making excuses like saying smoking helps with relaxation
questions.explanation: Salah satu cara mengurangi disonansi adalah mencari alasan untuk membenarkan perilaku, seperti alasan relaksasi.

questions.questionText: What does the Big Bang Theory explain?
questions.explanation: Teori Big Bang dijelaskan sebagai model ilmiah untuk menjelaskan asal-usul alam semesta.

questions.questionText: About how long ago did the Big Bang occur?
questions.explanation: Profesor menyebutkan bahwa alam semesta dimulai sekitar 13,8 miliar tahun yang lalu.

questions.questionText: What major evidence for the Big Bang was discovered in the 1960s?
options: A black hole in our galaxy
New planets outside our solar system
questions.explanation: Bukti terkuat adalah penemuan radiasi latar belakang gelombang mikro kosmik (CMB) pada 1960-an.

questions.questionText: To what does the professor compare this radiation?
options: A bright light in the sky
A faint echo of the initial explosion
The sound of a ringing bell
questions.explanation: Radiasi ini diibaratkan seperti 'gema samar' (faint echo) dari ledakan awal yang masih bisa dideteksi.

questions.questionText: What symptoms is the student experiencing?
questions.explanation: Mahasiswi tersebut mengatakan bahwa dia merasa 'sangat pusing dan lemas'.

questions.questionText: Why did the student refuse the 2 PM appointment?
options: She had a biology class until 3 PM
The bus doesn't run at that time
questions.explanation: Dia menolak karena ada kelas biologi yang berlangsung sampai jam 3 siang.

questions.questionText: At what time is her final appointment scheduled?
questions.explanation: Janji temunya akhirnya dijadwalkan pada jam 4 sore dengan Dr. Miller.

questions.questionText: What must the student do before her appointment?
options: Arrive 15 minutes early to fill out forms
questions.explanation: Resepsionis menginstruksikan untuk datang 15 menit lebih awal (jam 3:45) untuk mengisi formulir kesehatan.

questions.questionText: What is a hydrothermal vent?
options: A crack in the ocean floor that releases hot, mineral-rich water
A place where corals grow rapidly
A hole made by deep-sea miners
questions.explanation: Ventilasi hidrotermal didefinisikan sebagai retakan di dasar laut yang mengeluarkan air panas kaya mineral.

questions.questionText: Why is photosynthesis impossible at hydrothermal vents?
options: There is no sunlight reaching the deep ocean floor
The minerals kill all the plants
questions.explanation: Fotosintesis membutuhkan cahaya matahari, yang tidak bisa menjangkau dasar samudra yang dalam.

questions.questionText: What process do bacteria use to produce energy in this environment?
questions.explanation: Bakteri di lingkungan ini melakukan 'Kemosintesis' untuk mengubah energi kimia menjadi energi organik.

questions.questionText: How do giant tube worms survive without mouths or digestive systems?
options: They rely on bacteria living inside their tissues
They absorb minerals through their skin
They move to shallow water to feed
questions.explanation: Cacing tabung raksasa bergantung sepenuhnya pada bakteri kemosintetik yang hidup di dalam jaringan mereka.

questions.questionText: When was the telegraph perfected by Samuel Morse?
questions.explanation: Samuel Morse menyempurnakan telegraf pada tahun 1830-an.

questions.questionText: How does the telegraph transmit messages?
options: By sending sounds through the air
By sending electrical pulses through wires
questions.explanation: Telegraf bekerja dengan cara mengirimkan pulsa listrik melalui kabel.

questions.questionText: Why was the telegraph particularly important for railroads?
options: To allow stations to coordinate schedules and prevent accidents
questions.explanation: Telegraf memungkinkan koordinasi jadwal kereta secara instan guna mencegah kecelakaan.

questions.questionText: What event happened in 1861 mentioned in the lecture?
options: The completion of the first transcontinental telegraph line in the US
The end of the Industrial Revolution
questions.explanation: Pada 1861, jalur telegraf lintas benua pertama selesai dibangun di Amerika Serikat.

questions.questionText: Why does the student go to see the professor?
options: To ask for a better grade
To request an extension on a history paper
To get help finding research materials
To apologize for missing a class
questions.explanation: Mahasiswa tersebut menemui profesor untuk meminta waktu tambahan karena ada makalah sejarah yang tenggat waktunya hari Jumat.

questions.questionText: What reasons does the student give for not finishing the paper?
options: He forgot the due date and lost his notes
He had the flu and the campus library was closed
His computer crashed and deleted his file
He had to work extra hours at his job
questions.explanation: Dia menjelaskan: 'Saya terkena flu parah minggu lalu... dan perpustakaan kampus tutup.'

questions.questionText: What does the professor decide to do?
options: Give him an extra week to finish
Lower his grade for turning it in late
questions.explanation: Profesor mengatakan: 'Saya bisa memberi Anda perpanjangan 48 jam. Makalah Anda akan jatuh tempo pada Minggu malam.'

questions.questionText: What must the student submit to the professor tomorrow?
options: The first draft of the paper
A medical note from a doctor
A list of his library books
questions.explanation: Profesor meminta: 'Saya ingin Anda membawakan kerangka (outline) makalah Anda besok setelah kelas.'

questions.questionText: Why is the student at the employment office?
options: To look for an on-campus part-time job
To complain about her work hours
questions.explanation: Mahasiswi tersebut mengatakan: 'Saya mencari pekerjaan paruh waktu di kampus untuk semester ini.'

questions.questionText: What restricts the student's availability to work?
options: She has sports practice every evening
She goes home on the weekends
She has morning classes and afternoon chemistry labs
She does not want to work at night
questions.explanation: Dia menjelaskan jadwalnya: 'Saya ada kelas pagi setiap hari, dan lab kimia pada Selasa dan Kamis sore.'

questions.questionText: Why does the student prefer the gym desk job over the cafeteria job?
options: She is not very strong and the cafeteria requires heavy lifting
She has friends who work at the gym
questions.explanation: Staf menyebutkan pekerjaan kantin butuh angkat beban berat, dan mahasiswi menjawab: 'Saya tidak terlalu kuat, jadi di kantin mungkin akan sulit.'

questions.questionText: According to the staff member, what is an advantage of the gym desk job?
options: The manager allows employees to study when it's not busy
questions.explanation: Staf tersebut mengatakan: 'Bagian terbaik dari meja gym adalah ketika sedang sepi, manajer mengizinkan Anda untuk membaca atau mengerjakan pekerjaan rumah (belajar).'

questions.questionText: What problem are the two students discussing?
options: They cannot find a topic for their project
A group member is not responding or contributing
Professor Miller gave them a bad grade
questions.explanation: Mereka sedang mendiskusikan teman sekelompok (Greg) yang tidak merespons dan belum mengirimkan pekerjaannya untuk presentasi.

questions.questionText: What does the woman initially want to do about the problem?
options: Kick Greg out of the group
Tell Professor Miller that Greg isn't doing his share
questions.explanation: Mahasiswi tersebut berkata: 'Saya pikir kita sebaiknya memberi tahu Profesor Miller bahwa Greg tidak mengerjakan bagiannya.'

questions.questionText: What is the man's suggestion?
options: Try calling Greg tonight before going to the professor
questions.explanation: Mahasiswa pria menyarankan: 'Mari coba meneleponnya malam ini, dan jika dia tidak menjawab... baru kita jadwalkan pertemuan dengan Profesor.'

questions.questionText: What is the woman going to do as a precaution?
questions.explanation: Di akhir percakapan, mahasiswi itu mengatakan: 'Saya mulai mengumpulkan beberapa data cadangan untuk berjaga-jaga jika dia benar-benar keluar.'

questions.questionText: Why does the student meet with the academic advisor?
options: To apply for a summer internship
To change his major from Biology to Environmental Science
To ask for a letter of recommendation
questions.explanation: Mahasiswa tersebut berkata: 'Saya serius mempertimbangkan untuk mengubah jurusan saya dari Biologi ke Ilmu Lingkungan.'

questions.questionText: Why does the student want to change his major?
options: He realized he hates working indoors in a lab and prefers fieldwork
His friends are in Environmental Science
questions.explanation: Dia menyadari hal ini saat magang: 'Saya menyadari bahwa saya benci terjebak di dalam ruangan. Mahasiswa Ilmu Lingkungan yang saya temui melakukan banyak kerja lapangan.'

questions.questionText: What is the student's main concern about changing majors?
options: Delaying his graduation by an extra year
questions.explanation: Ketika ditanya apakah dia khawatir akan menunda kelulusannya, dia menjawab: 'Ya, tepat sekali. Saya tidak ingin menghabiskan waktu setahun ekstra di kampus.'

questions.questionText: What does the advisor tell him about his previous credits?
options: He will have to start over as a freshman
His foundational science courses will transfer over perfectly
He can only transfer his math credits
questions.explanation: Penasihat mengatakan kabar baiknya adalah: 'Kredit biologi, kimia, dan kalkulus pengantar Anda semuanya akan ditransfer dengan sempurna.'

questions.questionText: Why is the book the student needs unavailable?
options: The only copy was lost and it is an out-of-print book
It is kept in a restricted area
questions.explanation: Pustakawan menjelaskan: 'Satu-satunya salinan kita hilang beberapa tahun yang lalu, dan karena itu buku yang sudah tidak dicetak lagi (out-of-print), universitas belum bisa menggantinya.'

questions.questionText: What is the Interlibrary Loan system?
options: A place to buy cheap textbooks
A service where the library borrows a book from another university
A website to read digital books
questions.explanation: Pustakawan mendefinisikannya: 'Pada dasarnya kita meminjam buku tersebut dari universitas lain di jaringan kita yang memiliki salinannya.'

questions.questionText: How much does the Interlibrary Loan service cost the student?
options: It is a free service for students
questions.explanation: Ketika mahasiswi bertanya apakah ada biayanya, pustakawan menjawab: 'Ini adalah layanan gratis untuk mahasiswa.'

questions.questionText: How will the student know when the book arrives?
options: She has to check the catalog every day
She will receive an automated email notification
A letter will be sent to her dorm
questions.explanation: Pustakawan berkata: 'Anda akan mendapatkan pemberitahuan email otomatis segera setelah siap untuk diambil.'

questions.questionText: Why is the student talking to the advisor?
options: To drop a computer science class
To ask about adding a minor to his degree
To complain about an art professor
questions.explanation: Mahasiswa tersebut berkata di awal: 'Saya ingin berbicara tentang menambahkan minor pada gelar saya.'

questions.questionText: What is the student's current major?
questions.explanation: Penasihat akademik mengkonfirmasi: 'Anda saat ini mengambil jurusan Ilmu Komputer (Computer Science), kan?'

questions.questionText: Why does the student want to minor in Graphic Design?
options: He wants to become a full-time artist
He thinks it will make his web development projects look more professional
It is required for his major
His friends are taking graphic design classes
questions.explanation: Dia menjelaskan: 'saya pikir memahami prinsip-prinsip desain akan membuat aplikasi dan situs web yang saya bangun terlihat jauh lebih profesional.'

questions.questionText: What must the student do to be accepted into the Graphic Design minor?
options: Complete two intro art classes and submit a portfolio
Write an essay about art history
questions.explanation: Penasihat mengatakan: 'Anda harus menyelesaikan dua kelas seni pengantar terlebih dahulu, lalu menyerahkan portofolio karya Anda.'

questions.questionText: What is the student's problem?
options: Her roommate's guests are too noisy late at night
questions.explanation: Mahasiswi tersebut mengeluh: 'Dia terus membawa teman-temannya ke kamar asrama kami larut malam... Mereka berbicara keras dan memutar musik.'

questions.questionText: When does the roommate usually bring friends over?
questions.explanation: Teks secara spesifik menyebutkan: 'sangat larut malam, biasanya setelah tengah malam (after midnight).'

questions.questionText: What does the RA suggest they do first?
options: Move the student to a new room immediately
Hold a formal roommate agreement meeting
questions.explanation: RA menyarankan: 'Saya sarankan kita mengadakan pertemuan kesepakatan teman sekamar yang formal (formal roommate agreement meeting).'

questions.questionText: What will happen if the roommate violates the written rules?
options: She will be expelled from the university
The RA will help the student file a room transfer request
The RA will confiscate her speakers
The student will have to pay a fine
questions.explanation: RA menyatakan: 'Jika dia melanggar perjanjian tertulis setelah itu, saya dapat membantu Anda mengajukan permintaan transfer kamar.'

questions.questionText: Why did the student go to the professor's office?
options: To hand in his final paper
To discuss a low grade he received on his midterm essay
To ask for a letter of recommendation
questions.explanation: Mahasiswa itu berkata: 'Saya ingin bertanya tentang nilai esai tengah semester saya. Saya mendapat nilai C...'

questions.questionText: According to the professor, what was the main problem with the essay?
options: The thesis statement was too broad
questions.explanation: Profesor menjelaskan: 'Masalah utamanya di sini bukanlah gaya penulisan Anda; melainkan pernyataan tesis Anda. Terlalu luas (too broad).'

questions.questionText: What did the student spend too much time doing in the essay?
questions.explanation: Profesor mengatakan: 'Anda akhirnya hanya merangkum alur cerita daripada menganalisis aspek tertentu.'

questions.questionText: What does the professor suggest the student do for the next paper?
options: Bring the thesis and outline to her office or the writing center before writing
questions.explanation: Profesor merekomendasikan: 'saya sangat merekomendasikan Anda membawa tesis dan kerangka Anda ke jam kantor saya atau ke pusat penulisan kampus sebelum Anda mulai menulis.'

questions.questionText: What event are the students discussing?
questions.explanation: Mahasiswi bertanya: 'Apakah kamu akan pergi ke bursa kerja (career fair) universitas besok?'

questions.questionText: What is an 'elevator pitch'?
options: A 30-second introduction about oneself and one's skills
A conversation held inside an elevator
questions.explanation: Mahasiswa itu mendefinisikannya: 'Ini adalah pengenalan singkat 30 detik... Anda memberi tahu perekrut nama Anda, jurusan Anda, keterampilan utama Anda...'

questions.questionText: Why is it called an elevator pitch?
options: Because it helps you go up in your career
Because it should be short enough to say during a quick elevator ride
Because it was invented by an elevator company
Because recruiters interview people in elevators
questions.explanation: Dia menjelaskan: 'Disebut elevator pitch karena harus cukup pendek untuk diucapkan saat perjalanan singkat di dalam lift (elevator).'

questions.questionText: What advice does the man give regarding resumes?
options: Print them on high-quality resume paper
Don't bring any resumes to the fair
Handwrite the resumes for a personal touch
questions.explanation: Pria itu menyarankan: 'pastikan Anda mencetaknya di kertas resume berkualitas tinggi, bukan sekadar kertas printer standar.'

questions.questionText: What problem is the student facing?
options: She is being asked to pay fees to read academic articles off-campus
She doesn't know what topic to research
questions.explanation: Mahasiswi itu mengeluh: 'setiap kali saya mengklik artikel jurnal akademik, ia meminta saya untuk membayar biaya tiga puluh dolar!'

questions.questionText: Why do students normally get free access to these databases?
options: Because the university pays for subscriptions to those databases
questions.explanation: Pustakawan menjelaskan: 'Universitas membayar biaya langganan database tersebut, sehingga mahasiswa mendapatkan akses gratis.'

questions.questionText: What does the librarian tell the student to download to bypass the fees?
questions.explanation: Dia menginstruksikan: 'Anda hanya perlu mengunduh perangkat lunak VPN universitas ke laptop Anda.'

questions.questionText: How can the student filter out irrelevant or older articles in her search?
options: Use the 'advanced search' option to select 'Peer-Reviewed Only' and set a recent date
Ask the librarian to search for her
Use Google instead of the library system
questions.explanation: Pustakawan menyarankan: 'Gunakan selalu opsi pencarian lanjutan (advanced search). Anda bisa mencentang kotak yang bertuliskan 'Hanya Peer-Reviewed' dan mengatur tanggal publikasi...'

questions.questionText: Why is the student talking to the housing staff?
options: To complain about a broken heater in her room
To ask for an extra bed
questions.explanation: Mahasiswi tersebut mengatakan: 'Saya perlu melaporkan masalah dengan kamar asrama saya... Pemanasnya tidak berfungsi.'

questions.questionText: What happened when maintenance visited previously?
options: They refused to enter the room
They looked at it but the heater stopped working again after an hour
They told her she had to buy a new one
questions.explanation: Dia menjelaskan: 'Seorang pekerja pemeliharaan datang kemarin... berfungsi selama satu jam dan kemudian berhenti lagi.'

questions.questionText: What temporary solution does the staff offer?
options: A different room for the night
questions.explanation: Staf tersebut menawarkan: 'Sementara itu, saya bisa meminjamkan pemanas ruangan portabel (portable space heater) untuk Anda gunakan.'

questions.questionText: When will the maintenance team return to fix the problem?
questions.explanation: Staf mengatakan akan menjadikannya pesanan darurat dan 'Tim akan ke sana sore ini (this afternoon).'

questions.questionText: What is the main purpose of the conversation?
options: To argue about a test grade
To narrow down a topic for a presentation
questions.explanation: Mahasiswa itu meminta saran untuk topik presentasi akhirnya, dan profesor membantu mempersempitnya.

questions.questionText: What was the student's original topic idea?
questions.explanation: Mahasiswa tersebut berkata: 'Saya berpikir untuk melakukannya tentang tidur (sleep).'

questions.questionText: Why does the professor reject the original idea?
options: It is not related to psychology
It is too broad for a ten-minute presentation
Another student is already doing it
It is too difficult to research
questions.explanation: Profesor menjelaskan: 'topik itu terlalu luas (way too broad) untuk presentasi sepuluh menit.'

questions.questionText: What specific angle do they finally agree on?
options: The effects of sleep deprivation on short-term memory
questions.explanation: Mereka sepakat pada topik: 'Efek kurang tidur terhadap memori jangka pendek (The effects of sleep deprivation on short-term memory).'

questions.questionText: Why did the man miss the study group?
options: He mixed up the dates and thought it was tonight
questions.explanation: Pria itu berkata: 'Saya benar-benar mengacaukan tanggalnya. Saya pikir kita bertemu malam ini.'

questions.questionText: What subject is the study group for?
questions.explanation: Mahasiswi tersebut menyebutkan: 'kelompok belajar kimia (chemistry study group).'

questions.questionText: What does the woman offer to help the man?
options: She offers to do his homework
She offers to talk to the professor
She offers to let him copy her notes
She offers to delay the exam
questions.explanation: Wanita itu menawarkan: 'Saya bisa membiarkan kamu menyalin catatan saya jika kamu mau.'

questions.questionText: Where will they meet next time?
questions.explanation: Mereka sepakat untuk bertemu 'di kafe perpustakaan besok pagi (at the library café tomorrow morning).'

questions.questionText: Why does the student visit the professor?
options: To ask for a deadline extension
To ask about a research assistant position in his lab
questions.explanation: Mahasiswi itu melihat selebaran dan datang karena profesor 'mencari asisten peneliti sarjana untuk lab biologi laut.'

questions.questionText: What are the main duties of the position initially?
options: Entering data and feeding the lab fish
questions.explanation: Profesor menjelaskan tugasnya: 'memasukkan data ke dalam spreadsheet dan membantu memberi makan ikan dan membersihkan tangki.'

questions.questionText: How many hours a week is the commitment?
questions.explanation: Profesor mengatakan: 'Kami membutuhkan seseorang yang dapat berkomitmen sepuluh jam seminggu (ten hours a week).'

questions.questionText: What must the student do if she is still interested?
options: Email her resume and a cover letter by Friday
questions.explanation: Profesor memintanya: 'silakan email saya resume Anda dan surat pengantar singkat paling lambat hari Jumat.'

questions.questionText: Why did the student get a parking ticket?
options: He did not have a permit
He parked in the faculty lot instead of the student lot
questions.explanation: Mahasiswa itu mengakui: 'saya ditilang karena parkir di tempat parkir fakultas (faculty lot).'

questions.questionText: Why did he park in the wrong lot?
options: The student lot was completely full and he was late for a test
His friend told him to park there
questions.explanation: Dia beralasan: 'Lot C (parkir mahasiswa) benar-benar penuh!... Saya akan terlambat untuk ujian tengah semester fisika saya.'

questions.questionText: Can the officer cancel the ticket?
options: Yes, because he was late for a test
Yes, but only this one time
No, the rules are strict and he must pay the fine
No, but she will reduce the fine
questions.explanation: Petugas menegaskan: 'Kami tidak dapat membatalkan tiket untuk parkir di zona yang tidak sah. Anda tetap harus membayar denda.'

questions.questionText: What alternative does the officer suggest for the future if the lot is full?
options: Use the overflow gravel lot behind the stadium
questions.explanation: Petugas menyarankan: 'Anda diizinkan menggunakan tempat parkir kerikil limpahan (overflow gravel lot) di belakang stadion.'

questions.questionText: What is the main topic of the lecture?
options: The architecture of the Inca Empire
How the Inca communicated with neighboring tribes
How the Inca recorded information without a written language
The weapons used by the Inca military
questions.explanation: Profesor fokus membahas bagaimana bangsa Inca menggunakan sistem bernama 'quipu' untuk mencatat data karena mereka tidak memiliki bahasa tertulis.

questions.questionText: What is a quipu made of?
questions.explanation: Teks secara eksplisit menyebutkan bahwa quipu adalah sistem pencatatan yang 'seluruhnya terbuat dari tali yang disimpul (knotted strings).'

questions.questionText: What kind of information was primarily stored in a quipu?
options: Numerical data like census and taxes
questions.explanation: Profesor menyatakan bahwa bangsa Inca menggunakannya untuk 'mencatat data numerik. Misalnya... data sensus, kewajiban pajak, dan inventaris.'

questions.questionText: Why is the success of the Inca Empire surprising to historians?
options: They lived in a very cold climate
They had a very small population
They managed a massive, complex empire without a written language
They did not know how to farm
questions.explanation: Profesor mengatakan: 'Yang membuat keberhasilan mereka sangat mengejutkan... adalah bahwa mereka berhasil membangun dan mengelola masyarakat yang luas ini tanpa bahasa tertulis apa pun.'

questions.questionText: What is the main topic of the lecture?
options: The role of keystone species in maintaining an ecosystem
Why sea otters are hunted for their fur
The life cycle of giant kelp
questions.explanation: Kuliah tersebut membahas konsep 'spesies kunci' (keystone species) dan pentingnya peran mereka dalam suatu ekosistem.

questions.questionText: Why do ecologists use the term 'keystone'?
options: Because these animals live under rocks
Because like a stone in an arch, if removed, the whole system collapses
Because they are the heaviest animals in the ocean
Because they eat stones to help digestion
questions.explanation: Profesor membuat analogi dengan batu kunci pada lengkungan (arch): 'Jika Anda membuangnya, seluruh lengkungan akan runtuh.'

questions.questionText: In the professor's example, what do sea otters eat?
questions.explanation: Teks menyatakan: 'Berang-berang laut (sea otters) memakan bulu babi (sea urchins).'

questions.questionText: What happened to the kelp forest when the sea otters were hunted?
options: It grew faster than ever before
It was destroyed by an overpopulation of sea urchins
It washed up on the beach
questions.explanation: Ketika berang-berang laut diburu, populasi bulu babi meledak dan 'bulu babi memakan hutan rumput laut (kelp) hingga ke batuan dasar'.

questions.questionText: What is an exoplanet?
options: A planet that orbits the Sun
A planet outside of our solar system
questions.explanation: Profesor mendefinisikan eksoplanet sebagai: 'planet-planet di luar tata surya kita.'

questions.questionText: Why is it difficult for astronomers to see exoplanets directly?
options: They are too small to exist
They move too fast to photograph
The light from their host star is too bright
questions.explanation: Profesor menjelaskan bahwa kita jarang melihatnya langsung karena 'cahaya dari bintang induknya terlalu terang; hal itu membutakan teleskop kita.'

questions.questionText: What happens during a planetary transit?
options: A planet collides with its star
A star's brightness temporarily decreases because a planet passes in front of it
questions.explanation: Transit terjadi ketika 'sebuah planet lewat tepat di antara bintangnya dan teleskop kita... menghalangi sebagian kecil cahaya bintang tersebut.'

questions.questionText: What information can astronomers learn from measuring a transit?
options: The exact temperature of the planet
If the planet has aliens living on it
The size of the planet and how fast it orbits
The color of the planet's surface
questions.explanation: Teks menyatakan bahwa astronom dapat menghitung 'ukuran planet dan seberapa cepat ia mengorbit.'

questions.questionText: What is 'Planned Obsolescence'?
options: A strategy to make products last forever
A business strategy to intentionally design a product with a limited useful life
A plan to recall broken products
questions.explanation: Profesor mendefinisikannya sebagai: 'ketika sebuah perusahaan dengan sengaja mendesain sebuah produk dengan masa pakai yang terbatas.'

questions.questionText: What is the primary goal of this business strategy?
options: To force consumers to buy a replacement and increase sales
To create jobs in repair shops
questions.explanation: Tujuan utamanya adalah 'memaksa konsumen untuk membeli penggantinya, sehingga meningkatkan penjualan jangka panjang.'

questions.questionText: What example of this strategy does the professor mention regarding electronics?
options: Devices with batteries that cannot be easily replaced
questions.explanation: Contoh yang diberikan adalah 'menciptakan perangkat elektronik dengan baterai yang tidak dapat dengan mudah diganti oleh pengguna.'

questions.questionText: According to the lecture, what is a major negative consequence of planned obsolescence?
options: It forces companies to go bankrupt
It causes products to be too heavy
It creates a massive amount of electronic waste and harms the environment
It stops new inventions from being made
questions.explanation: Strategi ini dikritik keras karena 'menciptakan jumlah limbah elektronik yang sangat besar dan membahayakan lingkungan.'
